heading: Eligibility for participation
label: 2122 Eligibility for participation
notes: Amendments1989—Subsec. (a)(1). substituted “study or selected to receive specialized training” for “study, as that term is defined in ”.Pub. L. 101–189section 2120(3) of this title1987— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 100–180
amendments: Amendments1989—Subsec. (a)(1). substituted “study or selected to receive specialized training” for “study, as that term is defined in ”.Pub. L. 101–189section 2120(3) of this title1987— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 100–180

children.text: be accepted for admission to, or enrolled in, an institution in a course of study or selected to receive specialized training;

children.label: A
children.text: complete the educational phase of the program;

children.label: B
children.text: accept an appropriate reappointment or designation within his military service, if tendered, based upon his health profession, following satisfactory completion of the program;

children.label: C
children.text: participate in the intern program of his service if selected for such participation;

children.label: D
children.text: participate in the residency program of his service, if selected, or be released from active duty for the period required to undergo civilian residency if selected for such training; and

children.label: E
children.text: because of his sincere motivation and dedication to a career in the uniformed services, participate in military training while he is in the program, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense; and
meet the requirements for appointment as a commissioned officer.

children.label: b
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may require, as part of the agreement under subsection (a)(2), that a person must agree to accept, if offered, residency training in a health profession skill which has been designated by the Secretary as a critically needed wartime skill.
